tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for Preparation of Techno-Economic Feasibility Report (TEFR) for Development of Greenfield Airport at Nagarjuna Sagar, Andhra Pradesh
tenders.issuingAuthority: Andhra Pradesh Airports Development Corporation Ltd (APADCL)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee to be quoted (financial bid)
tenders.scopeSummary: Preparation of a Techno-Economic Feasibility Report for a proposed greenfield airport at Nagarjuna Sagar. Scope includes site delineation, site-specific surveys/studies, 35-year traffic forecasts, a concept master plan per latest guidelines, a financial model and project structuring, environmental & social impact studies, and facilitation of statutory approvals. Selection is CQCCBS (QCBS) with a 70:30 technical:financial weighting.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: At least 5 years of aviation consultancy experience in India (esp. to government entities), evidenced by minimum 2 similar aviation-sector projects in last 5 years; airport consulting (TEFR/DPR/Transaction Advisory) and technical consultancy credits scored separately
keyPersonnel: Project Director/Team Leader (PG/MBA/Transport Planner, 20 yrs, PPP projects >₹1000 Cr, ≥1 airport project)
Financial Specialist (MBA-Fin/CFA/CA, 10 yrs, airport/PPP financial modelling)
Social Impact Assessment Expert (≥5 SIA assignments >₹200 Cr)
other: Minimum annual turnover ₹500 Cr in each of last 3 FY (ending 31-03-2025)
Complete core team must be available
Consortium of max 3 members allowed

technicalCriteria.criterion: Bidder's experience providing consulting services (TEFR/DPR/Transaction Advisory) for airport projects in last 10 years
subCriteria: One airport project ≥₹1500 Cr, OR two airports ≥₹750 Cr each (5 marks/project)

technicalCriteria.criterion: Bidder's experience of consultancy for a greenfield airport
Bidder's experience providing technical services (DPR/master plan/detailed design/surveys) for airport projects in last 10 years
Successful completion of a transport infrastructure project on PPP basis in last 5 years (total cost ≥₹3000 Cr)

tenders.sourceDocument: APADCL RFP Notice No. 8 — TEFR for Greenfield Airport at Nagarjuna Sagar (2025)

tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for Preparation of Detailed Techno-Economic Report (DTER) for Development of Greenfield International Airport at Hosur, Krishnagiri District, Tamil Nadu
tenders.issuingAuthority: Tamil Nadu Industrial Development Corporation Ltd (TIDCO)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee to be quoted; contract period 120 weeks
tenders.scopeSummary: Preparation of a comprehensive Detailed Techno-Economic Report (DTER) for a greenfield international airport at Hosur, including market/O-D demand assessment, site-specific surveys, master planning, financial modelling, obtaining mandatory clearances, and bid process management for the development model. Selection is QCCBS (QCBS) 80:20.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Experience preparing TEFR/DPR (incl. master plan, financial assessment, site-specific surveys) for at least one airport of 10+ MPPA in last 15 years; Team Leader must have ≥1 transport infrastructure project ≥₹250 Cr
other: Minimum average annual turnover ₹100 Cr (best 3 of last 6 FY)
Team Leader must be from lead member of consortium
Parent-company turnover allowed only with ≥51% shareholding
No debarment subsisting on submission date

technicalCriteria.criterion: Experience preparing TEFR/DPR (master plan, financial assessment, surveys) for airport projects in last 15 years
Transaction Advisory services to Government/PSU for transport infrastructure in last 15 years
Approach, Work Plan & Methodology and Presentation

subCriteria: Team Leader – 5 (qualifying airport ≥10 MPPA = 3, extras up to 2)
Environmental Expert – 3 (EIA with EC from MoEF)
technicalCriteria.notes: Airport-experience scored by passenger-capacity qualifying projects

tenders.sourceDocument: TIDCO RFP — DTER for Greenfield International Airport at Hosur (Nov 2025)

tenders.title: Consultancy Services for Preparation of Techno-Economic Feasibility Report (TEFR) for Development of a New Greenfield Airport near Chennai, Tamil Nadu
tenders.issuingAuthority: Tamil Nadu Industrial Development Corporation Ltd (TIDCO)
tenders.scopeSummary: Preparation of a Techno-Economic Feasibility Report for a new greenfield airport near Chennai (Parandur), including survey & investigations, air-traffic forecasts, airport facility requirements, master layout plan, cost estimates, revenue/financial & economic analysis, environmental & social impact analyses, assistance in obtaining clearances, and bid process management for developer selection. Selection is QCBS 80:20.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Comprehensive consultancy for airport development projects in last 15 years; Team Leader must have completed consultancy as Team Leader for ≥1 airport project ≥₹1000 Cr
keyPersonnel: Project Director/Team Leader (20 yrs, ≥1 airport ≥₹1000 Cr)
other: Minimum average annual turnover ₹100 Cr in last 3 FY with positive net worth
Team Leader must be full-time employee of the (lead) bidder
Team Leader must be from lead member in a consortium

technicalCriteria.criterion: Firm's experience in comprehensive airport-development consultancy in last 15 years
subCriteria: Project ≥₹1000 Cr – base marks; additional projects ≥₹500 Cr – 5 marks each

technicalCriteria.criterion: Transaction Advisory to Government/PSU for transport infrastructure on PPP in last 15 years
subCriteria: Project ≥₹1000 Cr – 5 marks; additional ≥₹500 Cr – 2.5 marks each

technicalCriteria.criterion: Approach, Work Plan & Methodology and Presentation

technicalCriteria.notes: Each CV scored on qualifying airport project value (₹1000 Cr) plus additional projects

tenders.sourceDocument: TIDCO RFP — TEFR for New Greenfield Airport near Chennai (2020)

tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for Pre-feasibility Study & Preparation of Techno-Economic Feasibility Report (TEFR) for Development of Greenfield International Airport at Amaravati, Andhra Pradesh
tenders.issuingAuthority: Andhra Pradesh Airports Development Corporation Ltd (APADCL)
tenders.tenderRef: RFP Notice No.1/Amaravati Intl. Airport/APADCL/2025-26 dt. 11.04.2025
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee to be quoted; tender fee ₹59,000, EMD ₹5,00,000
tenders.scopeSummary: Pre-feasibility study and TEFR for a greenfield international airport in the Amaravati capital region, Guntur district. Scope covers site delineation, site-specific surveys, traffic demand assessment & forecasting, ICAO airport categorisation, financial model/cash-flow analysis, a parallel Environmental & Social Impact Assessment (EIA/SIA/R&R with Form-1/1A to SEAC/EAC), city-side/non-aero revenue study, land-procurement framework, and obtaining DGCA/MoCA/MoEF clearances. Selection is QCBS 70:30.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: At least 5 years of aviation consultancy experience in India (esp. to government entities), evidenced by minimum 2 similar aviation-sector projects in last 5 years
keyPersonnel: Project Director/Team Leader (PG/MBA/Transport Planner, 20 yrs, PPP projects >₹1000 Cr, ≥1 airport project)
Environmental Expert (M.Env, 10 yrs, EIA of projects >₹200 Cr with EC from MoEF)
other: Minimum annual turnover preferably ₹500 Cr in each of last 3 FY (ending 31-03-2024)
Complete core team must be available
Consortium of max 3 members allowed

technicalCriteria.criterion: Bidder's experience providing consulting services (TEFR/DPR/Transaction Advisory) for airport projects in last 10 years
subCriteria: One airport ≥₹1500 Cr OR two airports ≥₹750 Cr each

technicalCriteria.criterion: Bidder's experience of consultancy for a greenfield airport
Bidder's experience providing technical services (DPR/master plan/design/surveys) for airport projects in last 10 years
Successful completion of a transport infrastructure project on PPP (total cost ≥₹3000 Cr) in last 5 years

tenders.sourceDocument: APADCL RFP No.1/2025-26 — Pre-feasibility & TEFR for Greenfield International Airport at Amaravati (2025)

tenders.title: Selection of Consultant for Surveys/Investigations, Feasibility, OLS Survey, EIA & Statutory Approvals for Development of Water Aerodromes (Seaplane) at Eight Locations in Andhra Pradesh
tenders.issuingAuthority: Andhra Pradesh Airports Development Corporation Ltd (APADCL)
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Consultancy fee to be quoted (8 locations)
tenders.scopeSummary: Consultancy for developing water aerodromes / seaplane facilities at eight locations in Andhra Pradesh: site survey, geotechnical & bathymetric/hydrographic surveys, Obstacle Limitation Surface (OLS) survey per ICAO Annex 14 & DGCA CAR, CRZ mapping, jetty/runway siting, feasibility study, EIA/EMP with EC/CRZ clearance from MoEF&CC through a NABET-accredited EIA consultant, and statutory approvals. Selection is QCBS 60:40.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: DPR/feasibility consultancy for aerodromes/airports (each project ≥₹20 Cr cost or ≥₹2 Cr assignment) and EIA/EMP/CRZ assignments (≥₹20 Cr) at ports/airports/govt bodies in last 7 years; Team Leader ≥1 port/airport project >₹80 Cr
other: Average annual financial turnover criterion (last 3 FY, CA-certified)
EIA must be via MoEF&CC/NABET-accredited consultant
Sub-contracting only with prior APADCL approval
Outsourced personnel get only 75% of CV score

technicalCriteria.criterion: Skill and Competency of Team (key personnel CVs)

other: Open tender on eProcure (etenders.gov.in)

tenders.title: Appointment of Consulting Firm for Establishment of FFS (Full Flight Simulator) and TRTO Facility — Strategy, Procurement & Program Management Support at AAI Airports
tenders.scopeSummary: Transaction-advisory / strategy consultancy providing end-to-end advisory and documentation support to AAI for establishment, procurement, installation, commissioning and O&M planning of Full Flight Simulator (FFS) and Type Rating Training Organisation (TRTO) facilities. Structured in two phases: Phase 1 project strategy & configuration and Phase 2 program-management support. Selection is QCBS 80:20.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: At least 5 projects in project structuring / commercial & financial advisory / sector study / strategy in the aviation sector in last 7 years; plus ≥1 business-case / strategic-roadmap / transaction-advisory engagement for a central/state government aviation training institute in last 7 years
other: Registered legal entity in India, ≥10 years in existence
Minimum average annual turnover ₹300 Cr (FY24-25, 23-24, 22-23) from consultancy, CA-certified
Positive net worth
Foreign firms and consortium/JV not permitted

tenders.title: Obstruction (OLS) Survey and Allied Survey Works including Positioning of Survey Team at Cochin International Airport
tenders.estimatedValueLabel: Survey services fee to be quoted
tenders.scopeSummary: Aeronautical survey including OLS survey for Runway 09/27 at Cochin International Airport (with 1st and 2nd bi-annual surveys, plus validation/verification by AAI as per the AAI Aeronautical Survey Manual), 8 km topographic survey for fire & safety, and deployment of a survey team for aerodrome safeguarding (obstacle control & monitoring within 30 NM) over 6 years using GNSS/Total Station. This is a specialised surveyor procurement rather than a QCBS advisory selection.

eligibility.similarProjectsRequired: Executed similar OLS survey work at reputed airports (AAI/private) ≥₹50 lakh in last 5 years; completed OLS survey at 3 different airports in last years
keyPersonnel: Survey team (2 competent members) experienced in aerodrome safeguarding, GNSS/Total Station

evaluationFramework.summary:
Consultant selection for Indian airport & civil-aviation projects is done almost entirely through Quality & Cost Based Selection (QCBS) under GFR 2017 Rule 192 and the MoF Manual for Procurement of Consultancy Services (2022). Unlike the highly standardised road sector (uniform 80:20 + INFRACON), aviation shows a spread of technical:financial ratios: greenfield feasibility (TEFR/DTER) and strategy/transaction-advisory RFPs from TIDCO and AAI use 80:20 with a minimum technical score of 70/100, APADCL greenfield-airport TEFR RFPs use 70:30 with a 60/100 minimum, and multi-scope survey/EIA assignments (e.g. water aerodromes) use 60:40 with a 60% threshold. Financial bids of firms below the minimum technical score are returned unopened; the highest technical scorer is normalised to 100 and others pro-rated.

Technical scoring is dominated by two blocks: the firm's airport-consultancy track record and the qualifications of named key personnel, which together typically account for 60-80% of marks. A distinctive feature of aviation is that experience is measured by airport scale — passenger throughput (e.g. a qualifying project of 10+ million passengers per annum, additional airports of 5+ MPPA) and/or underlying project value (₹500 Cr / ₹750 Cr / ₹1000 Cr / ₹1500 Cr bands) — rather than the kilometre-based metrics used for roads. Transaction-advisory / PPP experience for transport infrastructure is scored as a separate sub-block because most greenfield airports are delivered on a PPP/DBFOT basis and the consultant must also run bid-process management. Approach, methodology and a formal technical presentation carry a meaningful 20-25 marks (larger than roads' typical 5).

Key-personnel scoring is CV-based against airport-specific roles: Team Leader/Project Director (20 yrs, must have led at least one large airport assignment ≥₹1000 Cr), Airport Sector Expert, Airport Planner (master planning), PPP/Transaction Advisory Expert, Financial Specialist (airport financial model), Environmental Expert (EIA with EC from MoEF&CC, often NABET-accredited), and Social/R&R Expert. Eligibility gates are financial (average annual turnover from consultancy ranging ₹100 Cr for greenfield TEFR up to ₹300-500 Cr for AAI advisory/large PMC) and experiential (typically ≥5 years of aviation consultancy and 2+ similar airport assignments). Specialised assignments add discipline gates: OLS/aeronautical surveys require AAI empanelment (GIS Circular 03/2021) and ICAO Annex 14 / DGCA CAR compliance; EIA requires a NABET/MoEF&CC-accredited consultant. There is no single mandatory personnel-registration portal equivalent to roads' INFRACON.
evaluationFramework.dominantMethod: QCBS (80:20 for greenfield feasibility & advisory; 70:30 and 60:40 also common)

criteria.label: Firm's Airport Consultancy Experience
criteria.description: Track record of airport consultancy — TEFR/DTER/DPR, master planning, detailed design and technical services — over the last 7-15 years. Uniquely to aviation, credit is driven by airport SCALE: passenger capacity of the served airport (a qualifying project of 10+ MPPA scores highest, additional airports of 5+ MPPA add marks) and/or underlying project value bands (₹500 Cr, ₹750 Cr, ₹1000 Cr, ₹1500 Cr). A separate credit is given for having done at least one greenfield airport.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Multiple airport assignments including at least one large (≥10 MPPA or ≥₹1500 Cr) airport plus additional 5+ MPPA airports, and prior greenfield airport experience
One qualifying large-airport assignment plus one or two additional airport projects of the required value
Meets the eligibility floor: 1-2 similar airport assignments in the relevant period
Below floor — no qualifying airport assignment or only sub-scale / non-airport transport work

inputFields.label: Number of airport consultancy assignments (TEFR/DPR/master plan) completed in last 10-15 years
inputFields.scoring: 1 = Fair, 2 = Good, 3+ = Excellent (eligibility usually needs ≥2 similar aviation projects)

inputFields.label: Largest airport served by capacity (million passengers per annum, MPPA)
inputFields.scoring: 10+ MPPA = full qualifying marks (e.g. 15); 5+ MPPA additional airports add ~5 each

inputFields.label: Highest underlying airport project value you have consulted on (₹)
inputFields.scoring: Scored against ₹1500/₹1000/₹750/₹500 Cr bands per the specific RFP

inputFields.label: Have you completed consultancy for at least one greenfield airport?
inputFields.scoring: Explicit standalone credit (e.g. 5 marks in APADCL RFPs)

criteria.label: PPP / Transaction Advisory Experience
criteria.description: Experience providing transaction advisory / bid-process management for transport infrastructure (airport, port, rail, road, metro) delivered on PPP basis to government or PSU clients. Scored separately because greenfield airports are usually developed via PPP/DBFOT concessions and the feasibility consultant runs the developer-selection bid process.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Multiple PPP transaction-advisory mandates including one ≥₹1000 Cr transport-infra project (ideally an airport) plus additional ≥₹250-500 Cr projects
One ≥₹500-1000 Cr transaction-advisory mandate plus one additional qualifying project
One qualifying PPP transaction-advisory assignment ≥₹500 Cr
No PPP/transaction-advisory experience for transport infrastructure

inputFields.label: Number of PPP transaction-advisory assignments for transport infrastructure (Government/PSU)
inputFields.scoring: First qualifying project = base marks; each additional ≥₹250-500 Cr project adds 2.5 marks (max per RFP)

inputFields.label: Largest transport-infra PPP project value advised (₹)
inputFields.scoring: APADCL also gives a discrete credit for completing a ≥₹3000 Cr PPP transport project

inputFields.label: Was any transaction-advisory mandate for an airport specifically?
inputFields.scoring: Airport-specific PPP advisory scores higher than generic transport-infra advisory

criteria.label: Approach, Methodology, Work Plan & Presentation
criteria.description: Quality of the technical approach, work plan and (in most aviation RFPs) a formal technical presentation to the evaluation committee / MoCA / AAI. Aviation gives this a larger share (20-25 marks, sometimes split 20 methodology + 20 presentation) than the road sector, reflecting the multi-disciplinary nature (traffic forecasting, master planning, financial modelling, clearances).

scoringGuidance.meaning: Strong site/market appreciation, realistic phased methodology across TEFR + surveys + EIA + bid management, clear work plan and staffing, compelling presentation
Sound methodology and work plan with a competent presentation; minor gaps
Generic methodology largely restating the ToR; weak presentation
Boilerplate approach, poor grasp of aviation-specific tasks

inputFields.label: Self-assessed quality of your approach & methodology
inputFields.scoring: Maps to guidance; typically ~20 marks

inputFields.label: Can you deliver a technical presentation to the committee (MoCA/AAI) on approach & methodology?
inputFields.scoring: Presentation is a separately scored block (5-20 marks) in most aviation RFPs

criteria.label: Qualifications & Competence of Key Personnel
criteria.description: The largest scoring block (25-55 marks). Each named expert's CV is scored on qualification and airport-specific experience (measured again by airport scale / project value). Core aviation roles: Team Leader/Project Director (~20 yrs, must have led ≥1 airport project, often ≥₹1000 Cr), Airport Sector Expert, Airport Planner (master planning), PPP/Contract Expert, Financial Specialist (airport financial model), Environmental Expert (EIA with EC from MoEF&CC), and Social/R&R Expert. Outsourced personnel or those with <2 years' tenure may receive only ~75% of CV marks; the Team Leader must be from the lead member of a consortium.

scoringGuidance.meaning: All core roles filled by experts with strong airport-specific experience; Team Leader has led a large (≥₹1000 Cr / 10+ MPPA) airport assignment; Airport Planner has multiple airport master plans
All required positions filled and qualified; most experts have at least one qualifying airport project
Team meets minimum qualifications but several experts light on airport-specific projects
One or more core positions unfilled/under-qualified or Team Leader lacks a qualifying airport assignment

inputFields.label: Team Leader / Project Director — number of airport projects led (≥₹1000 Cr or 10+ MPPA)
inputFields.scoring: Qualifying airport project = base marks (e.g. 3-8); each additional airport adds ~1 mark

inputFields.label: Team Leader — total professional experience (years)
inputFields.scoring: ≥20 yrs typically required for eligibility

inputFields.label: Airport Planner — number of airport master plans prepared
inputFields.scoring: 1 master plan = qualifying; additional master plans add ~1 mark each (max per RFP)

inputFields.label: Number of core airport roles you can staff with qualified CVs (of ~7: TL, Airport Sector Expert, Airport Planner, PPP/Contract Expert, Financial Specialist, Environmental Expert, Social Expert)
inputFields.scoring: Full team = full marks; each unfilled/under-qualified core role loses that CV's marks

inputFields.label: Environmental Expert has completed EIA of a project ≥₹200-250 Cr and obtained EC from MoEF&CC?
inputFields.scoring: Qualifying EIA project = ~2 marks; extras add up to max

inputFields.label: Average tenure of proposed key experts with your firm
inputFields.scoring: Outsourced or <2 yr tenure personnel scored at ~75% of CV marks (e.g. APADCL water aerodromes)

criteria.label: Financial Turnover & Eligibility (Gate)
criteria.description: Primarily an eligibility gate: minimum average annual turnover from consultancy, plus positive net worth and years in existence. Aviation thresholds are high and vary widely by assignment scale — around ₹100 Cr for greenfield TEFR/DTER (TIDCO), and ₹300-500 Cr for large AAI advisory/PMC assignments. Some RFPs also require the complete core team to be available at bid stage.

scoringGuidance.meaning: Average annual consultancy turnover comfortably exceeds the RFP threshold (e.g. > ₹500 Cr) with strong positive net worth
Turnover clears the threshold with reasonable margin
Turnover just meets the threshold (e.g. exactly ₹100 Cr)
Turnover below the RFP threshold — ineligible

inputFields.label: Average annual turnover from consultancy business (last 3 FY, ₹)
inputFields.scoring: Must meet RFP floor: ~₹100 Cr (greenfield TEFR) up to ₹300-500 Cr (AAI advisory/large PMC)

inputFields.label: Does the firm have positive net worth (audited)?
inputFields.scoring: Positive net worth is a common mandatory condition

inputFields.label: Number of years the firm has been in existence
inputFields.scoring: Some AAI advisory RFPs require ≥10 years; ≥5 years of aviation consultancy is common

inputFields.label: Years of aviation-sector consultancy experience (esp. to government)
inputFields.scoring: ≥5 years typically required as an eligibility gate

criteria.label: Specialised Survey / EIA / Aeronautical Capability
criteria.description: For feasibility, survey and EIA assignments, discipline-specific capability and accreditations: Obstacle Limitation Surface (OLS) / aeronautical surveys per ICAO Annex 14 & DGCA CAR (surveyors must be AAI-empanelled under GIS Circular 03/2021), hydrographic/bathymetric and geotechnical survey capability (water aerodromes), and EIA through a NABET / MoEF&CC-accredited consultant capable of obtaining Environmental Clearance. Applies mainly to feasibility/survey RFPs rather than pure advisory/PMC.

scoringGuidance.meaning: AAI-empanelled for aeronautical survey, in-house ICAO Annex 14 OLS capability, NABET-accredited EIA, and hydrographic/geotechnical survey capability with multiple prior projects
Holds the key accreditations and has 2-3 relevant survey/EIA projects
Meets minimum accreditation but limited prior projects, or relies on associates
Lacks required accreditation (AAI empanelment / NABET) — ineligible for the relevant task

inputFields.label: Are you AAI-empanelled for aeronautical/OLS survey (GIS Circular 03/2021)?
inputFields.scoring: Mandatory gate for OLS/aeronautical-survey scope

inputFields.label: Number of OLS / aeronautical surveys completed (ICAO Annex 14 / DGCA CAR)
inputFields.scoring: Some tenders require OLS at ≥3 different airports and ≥₹50 lakh similar work

inputFields.label: Do you have (or partner with) a NABET / MoEF&CC-accredited EIA consultant?
inputFields.scoring: Mandatory for EIA scope; EIA up to EC scores higher than EIA report only

inputFields.label: In-house hydrographic/bathymetric & geotechnical survey capability?
inputFields.scoring: Relevant to water-aerodrome / seaplane assignments; associate/outsourced may be scored lower

keyPolicies.name: Manual for Procurement of Consultancy & Other Services (MoF, Dept. of Expenditure, updated June 2022)
keyPolicies.relevance: Umbrella policy under GFR 2017 defining consultant selection systems (QCBS, LCS, SSS, QBS, FBS) that aviation RFPs implement; GFR Rule 192 caps technical weightage at 80%.

keyPolicies.name: MoCA — Guidelines for Setting up of Greenfield Airports
keyPolicies.relevance: Defines the greenfield airport approval process (Steering Committee, site clearance, TEFR requirement, PPP delivery with Viability Gap Funding) that every airport feasibility/TEFR consultancy must comply with.

keyPolicies.name: ICAO Annex 14 & DGCA CAR Section 4 (Aerodrome Design & Operations) / AAI Aeronautical Survey Manual
keyPolicies.relevance: Technical standards governing Obstacle Limitation Surfaces (OLS), aeronautical surveys and aerodrome safeguarding; AAI GIS Circular 03/2021 empanelment is a gate for OLS/aeronautical survey consultants.

keyPolicies.name: MoEF&CC EIA Notification 2006 / NABET accreditation
keyPolicies.relevance: Airports/aerodromes require Environmental Clearance; EIA must be prepared by a NABET/MoEF&CC-accredited consultant, a scored/gating criterion in feasibility and EIA RFPs.
